features.name: assume inside unreachable if branch
features.description: Code should include an assume statement placed inside an if branch that can never be executed due to contradictory register assignment
features.snippet:
reg initstate = 1;
always @(posedge clk) begin initstate <= 1; if (!initstate) assume(data != 0); end

features.name: conditional assume macro viaifdef formal
features.description: Code should include a macro definition that conditionally defines assume based on FORMAL being defined

features.name: memory array reg with address port
features.description: Code should include a memory array declaration with unpacked dimensions and a separate address port for reading
features.snippet:
reg [31:0] mem [0:31];
always @(posedge clk) data <= mem[addr];

features.name: register with contradictory initialization
features.description: Code should include a register that is initialized to 1 and unconditionally set to 1 in an always block, creating an always-true condition
features.snippet:
reg initstate = 1;
always @(posedge clk) begin initstate <= 1; if (!initstate) assume(data != 0); end
